title: Information Hazards & Publication Risk
description: Questions about whether publishing your research could enable harmful applications.

questions.text: If your research is published in full detail, could it provide a 'roadmap' that would meaningfully assist someone seeking to cause harm?
questions.helpText: Consider whether your published methods, results, and data — taken together — would give a malicious actor a significant advantage they wouldn't otherwise have.

signals.concernDescription: Your detailed publication could provide a roadmap for harmful applications. Consider responsible publication strategies.
You were unsure whether your publication could serve as a roadmap for harm. Consider discussing with colleagues or a security specialist.

questions.text: Does your research lower the expertise, cost, or access barriers for creating dangerous agents, weapons, or capabilities?
questions.helpText: Even beneficial research that simplifies complex processes may lower barriers if the same simplification applies to harmful applications.

signals.concernDescription: Your research lowers barriers for creating dangerous agents or capabilities. This is a dual-use information hazard.
You were unsure whether your research lowers barriers for harmful applications.

questions.text: Have you considered whether any aspect of your results should be published with restricted detail, or shared only through controlled channels?

options.label: Yes, and we plan to restrict some details
Yes, we considered it but decided full publication is appropriate

signals.concernDescription: You have not yet considered whether any aspects of your results should be published with restricted detail. Given the dual-use dimensions identified in this assessment, this is worth reflecting on.
